steps.title: Link awe to helping
steps.content: The researchers claim that awe can make people more connected to others and more likely to act altruistically. Their study sets up an awe condition, looking at tall trees, and a control condition, looking at a plain building, then measures helping behavior after pens are spilled.

steps.title: Choose the direct support
steps.content: Choice B describes survey language after the experiment, not whether awe caused more helping. Choice C would weaken the claim because it links reported awe to not helping. Choice D is about noticing the spilled pens, but noticing a problem is not the same as acting altruistically. Choice A gives the needed pattern: participants who looked at the trees helped pick up significantly more pens than participants who looked at the building. The answer is A.
